property.name: java.library.path
property.value: C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-17\bin;C:\WINDOWS\Sun\Java\bin;C:\WINDOWS\system32;C:\WINDOWS;C:\Windows\system32;C:\Windows;C:\Windows\System32\Wbem;C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\Windows\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files (x86)\NVIDIA Corporation\PhysX\Common;C:\Program Files\nodejs\;C:\Program Files\Docker\Docker\resources\bin;C:\WINDOWS\system32;C:\WINDOWS;C:\WINDOWS\System32\Wbem;C:\WINDOWS\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\WINDOWS\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files\Amazon\AWSCLIV2\;C:\Program Files\Git\cmd;C:\Program Files\NVIDIA Corporation\NVIDIA app\NvDLISR;C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-17\bin;C:\Program Files\apache-maven-3.9.14-bin\apache-maven-3.9.14\bin;C:\Users\divya\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;C:\Users\divya\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin;C:\Users\divya\AppData\Roaming\npm;C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-21\bin;;.

testcase.name: Created pet appears in sold list after status update
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Created pet appears in sold list after status update # src/test/resources/features/CrossEndpoint.feature:23 12:15:47.944 [INFO ] clients.BaseTest - BaseTest initialized. Base URL: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 12:15:47.948 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 12:15:47.962 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Creating pet name='HighValueBulldog' status='available' category='HighValueBulldog' 12:15:51.964 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Created pet with category: name=HighValueBulldog category=HighValueBulldog status=available Given I create a pet with name "HighValueBulldog" category "HighValueBulldog" and status "available" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iCreateAPetWithNameCategoryAndStatus(java.lang.String,java.lang.String,java.lang.String) 12:15:51.968 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:15:52.916 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Extracted petId=323099 And I extract the pet ID from the response # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iExtractThePetIdFromTheResponse() 12:15:52.938 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Updating pet id=323099 to status='sold' 12:15:55.082 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - PUT /pet — updated id=323099 to status=sold When I send a PUT request to update the pet status to "sold" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPutRequestToUpdateThePetStatusTo(java.lang.String) 12:15:55.086 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:15:55.090 [INFO ] clients.StoreClient - Fetching store inventory 12:15:57.232 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - GET /store/inventory response: {"sold":383,"d":1,"string":10,"Busy":1,"pending":91,"available":478,"Not Available":2,"solsd":1,"bold":1,"availabl":1,"unknown_status":2,"Sold":1,"{{statusP}}":1,"Available":1} When I send a GET request to fetch the store inventory # stepDefinitions.InventorySteps.iSendAGetRequestToFetchTheStoreInventory() 12:15:57.236 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:15:57.238 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Finding pets with status='sold' 12:15:59.746 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/findByStatus?status=sold — count: 383 When I send a GET request to find pets by status "sold" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAGetRequestToFindPetsByStatus(java.lang.String) 12:15:59.747 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:15:59.843 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Verified pet ID=323099 exists in 'sold' list And the created pet ID should be present in the "sold" pets list # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theCreatedPetIdShouldBePresentInThePetsList(java.lang.String)

testcase.name: Verify available pet count matches between inventory and findByStatus(TC2 - Inventory Analysis
testcase.system-out: Scenario: Verify available pet count matches between inventory and findByStatus # src/test/resources/features/InventoryAnalysis.feature:6 12:15:59.885 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 12:15:59.886 [INFO ] clients.StoreClient - Fetching store inventory 12:16:01.653 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - GET /store/inventory response: {" Not available":1,"sold":402,"sold ":1,"NOT available":1,"string":3,"special-dog":1,"pending":86,"available":462,"avalible":1,"available_smart":1,"PENDING":5,"In Heaven":1,"available, sold":1,"avaliable":1,"Available":1} When I send a GET request to fetch the store inventory # stepDefinitions.InventorySteps.iSendAGetRequestToFetchTheStoreInventory() 12:16:01.654 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:01.683 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - Inventory available count: 462 And I extract the available count from inventory # stepDefinitions.InventorySteps.iExtractTheAvailableCountFromInventory() 12:16:01.685 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Finding pets with status='available' 12:16:04.502 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/findByStatus?status=available — count: 462 When I send a GET request to find pets by status "available" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAGetRequestToFindPetsByStatus(java.lang.String) 12:16:04.521 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:04.562 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - Inventory available count: 462 | findByStatus count: 462 12:16:04.579 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - TC2 verified: findByStatus count (462) > 0 And the count of pets returned should match the inventory available count # stepDefinitions.InventorySteps.theCountOfPetsReturnedShouldMatchTheInventoryAvailableCount()

testcase.name: Create, retrieve, update and delete a pet #1(TC1 - Pet Lifecycle
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Create, retrieve, update and delete a pet # src/test/resources/features/PetLifecycle.feature:26 12:16:04.603 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 12:16:04.604 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Stored pet details: name=BuddyTest status=available Given I have a pet with name "BuddyTest" and status "available" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iHaveAPetWithNameAndStatus(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) 12:16:04.605 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Creating pet with name='BuddyTest' status='available' 12:16:06.653 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - POST /pet response: {"id":469249,"name":"BuddyTest","photoUrls":[],"tags":[],"status":"available"} When I send a POST request to create the pet # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPostRequestToCreateThePet() 12:16:06.654 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:06.675 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Extracted petId=469249 And I extract the pet ID from the response # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iExtractThePetIdFromTheResponse() 12:16:06.677 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=469249 12:16:08.702 [WARN ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/469249 returned 404 on attempt 1. Retrying... 12:16:10.212 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=469249 12:16:12.501 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/469249 succeeded on attempt 2 12:16:12.501 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/469249 final response: {"id":469249,"name":"BuddyTest","photoUrls":[],"tags":[],"status":"available"} When I send a GET request to retrieve the pet by ID # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAGetRequestToRetrieveThePetByID() 12:16:12.503 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) And the pet name should be "BuddyTest" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.thePetNameShouldBe(java.lang.String) And the pet status should be "available" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.thePetStatusShouldBe(java.lang.String) 12:16:12.538 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Updating pet id=469249 to status='sold' 12:16:14.125 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - PUT /pet — updated id=469249 to status=sold When I send a PUT request to update the pet status to "sold" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPutRequestToUpdateThePetStatusTo(java.lang.String) 12:16:14.127 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:14.128 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Deleting pet with id=469249 12:16:16.176 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - DELETE /pet/469249 — status code: 200 When I send a DELETE request to delete the pet # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendADeleteRequestToDeleteThePet() 12:16:16.177 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:16.178 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=469249 12:16:18.120 [WARN ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Known Petstore API issue: pet 469249 still returns 200 after delete And the deleted pet should not be retrievable # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theDeletedPetShouldNotBeRetrievable()

testcase.name: Create, retrieve, update and delete a pet #2(TC1 - Pet Lifecycle
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Create, retrieve, update and delete a pet # src/test/resources/features/PetLifecycle.feature:27 12:16:18.134 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 12:16:18.135 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Stored pet details: name=MaxTest status=pending Given I have a pet with name "MaxTest" and status "pending" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iHaveAPetWithNameAndStatus(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) 12:16:18.136 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Creating pet with name='MaxTest' status='pending' 12:16:19.963 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - POST /pet response: {"id":451015,"name":"MaxTest","photoUrls":[],"tags":[],"status":"pending"} When I send a POST request to create the pet # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPostRequestToCreateThePet() 12:16:19.965 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:19.991 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Extracted petId=451015 And I extract the pet ID from the response # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iExtractThePetIdFromTheResponse() 12:16:19.992 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=451015 12:16:22.332 [WARN ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/451015 returned 404 on attempt 1. Retrying... 12:16:23.847 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=451015 12:16:26.209 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/451015 succeeded on attempt 2 12:16:26.210 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/451015 final response: {"id":451015,"name":"MaxTest","photoUrls":[],"tags":[],"status":"pending"} When I send a GET request to retrieve the pet by ID # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAGetRequestToRetrieveThePetByID() 12:16:26.211 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) And the pet name should be "MaxTest" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.thePetNameShouldBe(java.lang.String) And the pet status should be "pending" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.thePetStatusShouldBe(java.lang.String) 12:16:26.241 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Updating pet id=451015 to status='sold' 12:16:27.851 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - PUT /pet — updated id=451015 to status=sold When I send a PUT request to update the pet status to "sold" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPutRequestToUpdateThePetStatusTo(java.lang.String) 12:16:27.852 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:27.853 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Deleting pet with id=451015 12:16:29.424 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - DELETE /pet/451015 — status code: 200 When I send a DELETE request to delete the pet # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendADeleteRequestToDeleteThePet() 12:16:29.426 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:29.427 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=451015 12:16:31.841 [WARN ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Known Petstore API issue: pet 451015 still returns 200 after delete And the deleted pet should not be retrievable # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theDeletedPetShouldNotBeRetrievable()

testcase.name: Create a user with an invalid email format(TC3 - User Security and Error Handling
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Create a user with an invalid email format # src/test/resources/features/UserSecurity.feature:12 12:16:31.859 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 12:16:31.865 [INFO ] clients.UserClient - Creating user username='testUser001' email='invalid_email' 12:16:33.889 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - POST /user — username=testUser001 email=invalid_email status=200 When I send a POST request to create a user with username "testUser001" email "invalid_email" and password "pass123" # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.iSendAPostRequestToCreateAUser(java.lang.String,java.lang.String,java.lang.String) Then the create user response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.theCreateUserResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int)

testcase.name: Fetch a user that does not exist(TC3 - User Security and Error Handling
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Fetch a user that does not exist # src/test/resources/features/UserSecurity.feature:21 12:16:33.901 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 12:16:33.901 [INFO ] clients.UserClient - Fetching user with username='nonExistentUser12345' 12:16:35.730 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - GET /user/nonExistentUser12345 — status=404 When I send a GET request to fetch user "nonExistentUser12345" # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.iSendAGetRequestToFetchUser(java.lang.String) 12:16:35.731 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 404 Then the response status code should be 404 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:35.735 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - Response message verified: 'User not found' And the response message should contain "User not found" # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.theResponseMessageShouldContain(java.lang.String)

testcase.name: Login with incorrect credentials(TC3 - User Security and Error Handling
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Login with incorrect credentials # src/test/resources/features/UserSecurity.feature:30 12:16:35.742 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 12:16:35.743 [INFO ] clients.UserClient - Logging in with username='wrongUser' 12:16:37.474 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - GET /user/login — username=wrongUser status=200 When I send a GET request to login with username "wrongUser" and password "wrongPassword" # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.iSendAGetRequestToLoginWithUsernameAndPassword(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) 12:16:37.476 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 12:16:37.477 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - Login response status: 200 12:16:37.477 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - Login response body: {"code":200,"type":"unknown","message":"logged in user session:1776062801033"} 12:16:37.479 [WARN ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - KNOWN API LIMITATION: Petstore /user/login returns a session token even for invalid credentials. In a real system this would return 401 Unauthorized. 12:16:37.480 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - TC3 login negative test completed — API limitation documented And the response should not contain a valid session token # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.theResponseShouldNotContainAValidSessionToken()
